description: Tracks the carbon footprint of GreenCloud infrastructure. Shows real-time grid carbon intensity from Electricity Maps, estimated power consumption, cumulative CO2 emissions, and carbon-aware scheduling decisions.

options.content:
### How to read this dashboard

**Grid Carbon Intensity** shows how clean the electricity grid is right now (lower = cleaner). When it's green (<100), all builds proceed. When amber (100-300), dev builds are deferred. When red (>300), only production deploys go ahead.

**Power Draw** is estimated from CPU usage. **Emissions** = power consumed x grid carbon intensity over time. **Builds Deferred** counts how many non-critical builds were delayed to wait for cleaner energy.

panels.title: Grid Carbon Intensity (gCO2eq/kWh)
panels.description: How much CO2 is emitted per kWh of electricity generated right now. Data from Electricity Maps for your configured zone. Green = clean grid, Red = fossil-heavy grid.

panels.title: Carbon Status
panels.description: Current scheduling status. LOW = all builds proceed. MEDIUM = non-critical builds deferred. HIGH = only production deploys allowed.

panels.title: Total Power Draw
panels.description: Estimated total power consumption across all GreenCloud nodes (Pi 5 + Mini PC). Based on CPU usage: idle ~4W, full load ~12W for the Pi.

panels.title: Carbon Intensity Over Time (gCO2eq/kWh)
panels.description: Shows how the grid's carbon intensity changes throughout the day. Typically lower at night and when wind/solar generation is high. Useful for understanding when your infrastructure runs cleanest.

panels.title: Power Usage by Node
panels.description: Power draw per node. The Pi 5 runs 24/7 (4-12W). The Mini PC only draws power during builds (15-65W) and sleeps otherwise.

panels.title: Total Emissions (gCO2)
panels.description: Cumulative CO2 emitted since tracking began. Calculated as: energy consumed (kWh) x grid carbon intensity (gCO2/kWh) integrated over time.

panels.title: Emissions Today (gCO2)
panels.description: CO2 emitted today so far. For context: a typical GreenCloud day emits roughly 0.5-2g CO2 — equivalent to driving a car about 5 metres.

panels.title: Builds Deferred
panels.description: Number of non-critical builds that were delayed because the grid was too carbon-intensive. These builds ran later when the grid was cleaner, saving emissions.

title: GreenCloud Sustainability
